tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying etiology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
